# Pre-Implementation Critical Checklist: CRUD de Organizações Master

**Purpose**: Validate whether the Organization CRUD spec, plan, and tasks are clear, consistent, scoped, and ready for implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the primary CRUD requirements complete for create, list, get by ID, full update, and delete operations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002, Spec §API Endpoints]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required create semantics clear that organization and address data are accepted in one request and persisted as one logical operation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Spec §POST /backoffice/organizations]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required update semantics clear that `PUT` is a full replacement and updates organization and address together? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Spec §PUT /backoffice/organizations/:id]
- [x] CHK004 Are standalone address endpoints explicitly excluded from the feature scope? [Completeness, Spec §FR-041, Spec §Out of Scope]
- [x] CHK005 Are all expected error categories specified, including invalid payload, invalid CNPJ/CNAE/CEP/UF, missing plan, missing organization, duplicate CNPJ, and unexpected errors? [Completeness, Spec §Expected Errors, Spec §FR-037-FR-040]

## Domain Model Quality

- [x] CHK006 Is `Organization` explicitly defined as the Aggregate Root in the requirements and reinforced by the plan/tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Target Architecture, Tasks T007]
- [x] CHK007 Is `Address` explicitly defined as a persistable Entity and not an Aggregate Root across spec, plan, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §Address, Tasks T006]
- [x] CHK008 Is `SubscriptionPlan` clearly reused as an existing entity rather than recreated for this feature? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022-FR-023, Plan §Current Architecture Analysis, Tasks T011]
- [x] CHK009 Are `Cnpj` normalization, uniqueness, length, and validation requirements specific enough for implementation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014-FR-016, Data Model §Cnpj, Tasks T004/T021]
- [x] CHK010 Are `Cnae` normalization and structural-only 7-digit validation requirements specific enough, including no official database lookup? [Clarity, Spec §FR-017-FR-021, Data Model §Cnae, Tasks T005/T022]
- [x] CHK011 Is `secondaryCnaes` clearly specified as optional input with empty-list behavior when absent? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019-FR-020, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §Request Models]
- [x] CHK012 Is `currentPlanId` clearly required to reference an existing `SubscriptionPlan` for create and update flows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022, Spec §Expected Errors, Tasks T017/T034]
- [x] CHK013 Is CNPJ uniqueness clearly required for create and update without ambiguity around normalized values? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-039, Tasks T010/T017/T034]

## Layering & Architecture Consistency

- [x] CHK014 Are domain-layer boundaries specified so domain behavior remains independent of Fastify, Prisma, Zod, HTTP, and persistence concerns? [Consistency, Plan §Target Architecture, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK015 Are application-layer boundaries specified so use cases depend on repository ports rather than Prisma directly? [Consistency, Plan §Target Architecture, Data Model §Repository Port Target, Tasks T009-T011]
- [x] CHK016 Are infrastructure responsibilities clearly assigned to Prisma repositories, mappers, and factories? [Completeness, Plan §Target Architecture, Plan §Project Structure, Tasks T012-T016]
- [x] CHK017 Are presentation responsibilities clearly assigned to Fastify routes/handlers, presenters, and Zod schemas? [Completeness, Plan §Target Architecture, Tasks T018-T020, Tasks T029-T030, Tasks T035-T040]

## Scope Boundaries

- [x] CHK018 Is the absence of real authorization, permission middleware, Master profile validation, login work, and mandatory 403 behavior explicit and consistent? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Out of Scope, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK019 Are frontend, authentication, patients, users, billing, payment, logo upload, settings, and plan-history exclusions explicit enough to prevent scope expansion? [Completeness, Spec §FR-042, Spec §Out of Scope, Tasks §Scope Guardrails]
- [x] CHK020 Are tenant-operational data boundaries clear enough that responses are limited to organization registration data, address, and current plan summary? [Coverage, Spec §FR-043, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Task Readiness

- [x] CHK021 Are tasks small, objective, and tied to concrete files with objective, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and tests? [Completeness, Tasks T001-T052]
- [x] CHK022 Is the implementation order safe, with shared domain/repository/infrastructure foundations before story-specific use cases and routes? [Consistency, Tasks §Phase Dependencies, Tasks T004-T016]
- [x] CHK023 Is task overlap controlled enough that repeated files are split by endpoint/schema concern rather than broad generic CRUD work? [Clarity, Tasks T019/T029/T035, Tasks T020/T030/T036/T040/T043]
- [x] CHK024 Are minimum tests planned for value objects, entities, use cases, schemas, optional HTTP integration, typecheck, lint, build, and manual quickstart validation? [Completeness, Tasks T021-T026, T031-T033, T037-T038, T041-T052]

## Rollback & Migration Readiness

- [x] CHK025 Is rollback planning documented and are Prisma migrations planned only when schema or relation behavior actually requires them? [Completeness, Plan §Rollback Strategy, Plan §Validation Commands, Tasks T008/T046]

## Classification

**Result**: Pode implementar agora.

**Required adjustments before implementation**: None.

**Rationale**: The spec, plan, and tasks cover the requested domain boundaries, endpoint surface, validation rules, error handling, scope exclusions, rollback strategy, and test plan. The existing known implementation risk around CEP validation is already captured as a domain adjustment task rather than a planning blocker.

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: API requirements quality, domain modeling, layering, task readiness, rollback/migration readiness.
- Depth level: Critical pre-implementation gate.
- Actor/timing: Reviewer before implementation.
- Explicit must-have items incorporated: all 25 validation points from the user request.
